pattern: The coordinate range is tiny, so collect every integer point covered by at least one disk into a set and return its size.
hints: Coordinates and radii are small, so you can afford to test integer points directly rather than reason geometrically.
A point lies in a circle when the squared distance to its center is at most the squared radius, which keeps everything in integers.
Add every covered lattice point to a set across all circles and count the distinct entries.

edge_inputs.description: two overlapping circles, no double counting
minimum radius circle covers five points
identical circles must not double count
circle touching the axis edges of its allowed region
points exactly on the circumference count as inside

stress.description: several random circles within the allowed coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, min(size, 200))
    circles = []
    for _ in range(k):
        x = rng.randint(1, 100)
        y = rng.randint(1, 100)
        r = rng.randint(1, min(x, y))
        circles.append([x, y, r])
    return [circles]
stress.note: radius bounded by min(x, y) keeps every circle inside the positive coordinate region per the constraints
